old_claim.note: Reed Mat Architecture provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because generic windbreak shelters can use brush, hides, stakes, reeds, or mats without requiring the later southern Mesopotamian reed-architecture tradition.

source_shape.source_locator: Historical discussion of mudhif and reed architecture as an ancient southern Mesopotamian architectural tradition.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source scopes reed architecture to a specific Mesopotamian tradition, which is narrower and later than generic windbreak shelter technologies.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The old edge made one regional reed-building tradition a prerequisite for all early windbreak shelters, even though the dependent node is deliberately broader.

ontology_before: Windbreak shelters depended on a later, narrower reed-architecture node.
ontology_after: Windbreak shelters remain broad shelter technology and no longer inherit a regional reed-architecture prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-10-ancient-materials-scope keeps this removed edge absent and locks the corrected early-material chronology.
would_reject_if: The dependent node were narrowed to a later application that truly requires the removed prerequisite.
A stronger source showed the removed prerequisite existed earlier than and was necessary for the dependent node scope.
A future graph split introduced a narrower downstream node where this removed edge would be correctly scoped.
short_reason: No direct dependency remains because generic windbreak shelters can use brush, hides, stakes, reeds, or mats without requiring the later southern Mesopotamian reed-architecture tradition.
